page: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. No GPUs are included.

Benchmark data highlights the memory subsystem as a key performance driver. The r8g.8xlarge achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth tests, recording 402.49 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 183.47 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Passmark memory and database operations benchmarks also rank in the top 10% tier, with database operations reaching 47,337.28. Multi-core performance is strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,614.0. Single-core performance and integer processing tasks, such as Gzip compression and Clang software compilation, fall into the top 25% tier.

The instance exhibits certain performance tradeoffs, particularly in large-scale machine learning tasks. While LLM inference for small and medium models yields average performance, prompt processing for large 70B models falls into the bottom 25% tier at 5.9 tokens/sec. Qualitatively, the arm64 architecture and high memory-to-core ratio offer resource density and memory efficiency for scale-out workloads. The server is suited for memory-intensive applications, including high-throughput Redis deployments, relational databases, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the 64-bit ARM-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz and 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the r8g.8xlarge delivers top-tier memory performance, achieving 402.49 GB/sec read-only and 183.47 GB/sec write bandwidth in large-block tests, alongside a top-tier Passmark database operations score. While multi-core processing is strong, large-scale LLM inference represents a performance tradeoff, with prompt processing for 70B models falling into the lower tier. This instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, Redis caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, suited for databases and caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 32 Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance delivering high-bandwidth memory performance and dedicated core processing for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 cores at 2.8 GHz.
Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core.
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 402.49 GB/sec read and 183.47 GB/sec write speeds.
Delivers 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPUs.
Optimized for database operations, Redis deployments, and memory-bound applications.
